subject: ap-psychology
theme: Mental and physical health examines how psychological disorders are classified and explained (biomedical and biopsychosocial models), major disorder categories (anxiety, mood, schizophrenia spectrum, trauma), evidence-based treatments, and the bidirectional relationship between stress and physical health.

core_concepts.title: DSM-5 & Diagnostic Models
core_concepts.detail: The DSM-5 provides standardized diagnostic criteria based on symptom patterns and functional impairment. The biomedical model views disorders as diseases with biological causes; the biopsychosocial model integrates biological, psychological, and social factors. The diathesis-stress model proposes that disorders emerge from the interaction of a pre-existing vulnerability with environmental stressors.

core_concepts.title: Major Mood Disorders
core_concepts.detail: Major mood disorders: major depressive disorder (MDD) — 5+ depressive symptoms for 2+ weeks, no manic history; bipolar I — at least one manic episode; bipolar II — hypomanic + depressive episodes. Beck's cognitive triad of depression: negative views of self, world, and future, maintained by cognitive distortions (overgeneralization, all-or-nothing thinking, catastrophizing).

core_concepts.title: Anxiety & Trauma-Related Disorders
core_concepts.detail: Anxiety and trauma-related disorders: generalized anxiety disorder (chronic, uncontrollable worry); panic disorder (recurrent unexpected panic attacks); obsessive-compulsive disorder (intrusive obsessions + compulsions); post-traumatic stress disorder (intrusive memories, avoidance, hyperarousal, negative cognitions after trauma). Avoidance maintains anxiety by preventing extinction of conditioned fear.

core_concepts.title: Schizophrenia Spectrum
core_concepts.detail: Schizophrenia spectrum: positive symptoms (excesses — hallucinations, delusions, disorganized speech/behavior) vs. negative symptoms (deficits — flat affect, alogia, avolition, anhedonia). Diagnosis requires ≥2 symptoms for ≥6 months with functional decline. Dopamine hyperactivity in mesolimbic pathway is implicated in positive symptoms.

core_concepts.title: Psychological Treatments
core_concepts.detail: Psychological treatments: psychoanalytic — free association, dream analysis, transference interpretation, resistance; humanistic/client-centered — unconditional positive regard, empathy, genuineness; CBT — identifies and restructures maladaptive cognitions + behavioral activation; behavioral — systematic desensitization (graduated exposure + relaxation), aversion therapy (pairing unwanted behavior with aversive stimulus), token economies (operant reinforcement in institutional settings), applied behavior analysis (ABA); group therapy — provides social support, modeling, and shared experience at lower cost than individual therapy.

core_concepts.title: Biomedical Treatments
core_concepts.detail: Biomedical treatments: antidepressants — SSRIs block serotonin reuptake (fluoxetine, sertraline); MAOIs block serotonin/NE breakdown; SNRIs block both. Antipsychotics — typical/first-generation block D2 dopamine receptors; risk of tardive dyskinesia (late-onset involuntary movements). Mood stabilizers — lithium for bipolar disorder. Anxiolytics — benzodiazepines enhance GABA (short-term only; dependence risk). Electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) — effective for severe treatment-resistant depression.

core_concepts.title: Stress, Appraisal & Physical Health
core_concepts.detail: Stress and health: Selye's general adaptation syndrome (GAS) — alarm (fight-or-flight mobilization) → resistance (sustained coping) → exhaustion (depleted resources, vulnerability to illness). Chronic stress suppresses immune function, increasing infection susceptibility and slowing wound healing. Coping strategies include problem-focused coping (addressing the stressor directly) and emotion-focused coping (managing the emotional response). Burnout — a state of physical and emotional exhaustion — results from prolonged occupational stress.

exam_tip: FRQ questions on treatment require matching the specific therapy type to the correct theoretical basis — cognitive-behavioral therapies require naming the cognitive distortion OR the behavioral technique (e.g., ERP), not just 'therapy.'
